login.title: Sign in to OneBrain
login.subtitle: Enter your email to receive a magic link

magic_link.sent: We sent you a magic link. Check your inbox.

magic_link.success: You have been signed in successfully.
magic_link.email_body: Here is your magic link to sign in to OneBrain.rocks:

verify.title: Magic Link Verification
verify.success: You have been signed in successfully. Redirecting...

verify.missing_token: No verification token found. Please check your magic link.

auth.session_expired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.

register.title: Create Account

register.email_exists: An account with this email already exists.

2fa.setup_desc: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app and enter the code below.

2fa.enter_code: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
2fa.validate_desc: Enter the code from your authenticator app to complete sign in.

region.eu_description: Data stored in EU data centers. GDPR compliant.
region.global_description: Data stored in global data centers.

dev_tools.title: Dev-Tools
dev_tools.subtitle: SDK, REST API reference, and code examples for integrating OneBrain
dev_tools.sdk_desc: Zero-dependency TypeScript SDK with full type safety. Works in Node.js, Deno, and Bun — ESM and CommonJS.
dev_tools.api_desc: Direct HTTP access to all OneBrain endpoints. Use with any language or tool that supports HTTP requests.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.quickstart_text: Connect your AI and let it automatically learn from your past conversations — secure, private, and deletable anytime.

memory.title: Memories
memory.empty: No memories yet. Start adding your first memory.
memory.content_placeholder: What do you want to remember?
memory.placeholder_body: What do you want to remember?
memory.conflicts_desc: These memories have similar topics but different content. Resolve them below.
memory.duplicates_desc: Memories with similar titles that may be duplicates.

ingest_advanced.ai_extract_desc: Use AI to extract structured memories from text.
ingest_advanced.upload_desc: Upload .txt, .csv, or .json files to create memories.
ingest_advanced.upload_drop: Drag & drop a file here, or click to browse
ingest_advanced.chat_desc: Paste a chat transcript to extract memories from user messages.

entities.title: Entities
entities.name: Name
entities.graph_empty: No entities to display in graph.
entities.graph_click_hint: Click an entity node to see details.
entities.merge_confirm: Merge these entities? Links from the removed entity will be transferred.
entities.auto_extract_desc: Automatically extract entities from memory text.

api_keys.title: API Keys
api_keys.name: Name
api_keys.copy_warning: Copy this key now. It won't be shown again.
api_keys.confirm_revoke: Are you sure you want to revoke this key?

brain.title: Brain Profile
brain.summary_placeholder: Example: You are a full stack developer with 30 years of programming experience, specializing in TypeScript, React and Node.js...
brain.context_preview_desc: This is what AI tools see when they query your brain.
brain.rollback_confirm: Rollback to this version? All current active memories will be archived and replaced with the snapshot.

ingest.title: Import Knowledge
ingest.subtitle: Paste a chat conversation or text to extract memories from it.
ingest.paste_placeholder: Paste your chat transcript or any text here...
ingest.no_results: No memories extracted yet. Paste text above and click Extract.
ingest.manual_placeholder: What do you want to remember?
ingest.bulk_subtitle: Paste text below, then segment it into individual memories.
ingest.no_cards: Click the button above to segment your text into memory cards.
ingest.empty_text_warning: Please paste some text before creating memory cards.
ingest.candidates_desc: Memories suggested by your AI tools. Approve to keep, dismiss to remove.
ingest.no_candidates: No pending memories. When your AI tools learn something new, it will appear here for your review.

integrations.title: Connect Your AI Tools
integrations.subtitle: Connect any AI tool to your brain in under 2 minutes.
integrations.step1_desc: You need an API key to authenticate. Create one or use an existing key.
integrations.step2_desc: Pick your AI tool below, copy the config, and you're done.
integrations.connect_desc: The simplest way to connect any AI to your brain. One URL, paste it into any AI tool's custom instructions — done.
integrations.connect_ready: Your API key is ready. Use it with the Authorization header to access your brain context.
integrations.connect_step1: Copy the auth header or curl command above
integrations.connect_step2: Open your AI tool (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.) and go to Settings or Custom Instructions
integrations.connect_step3: Use the API key in the Authorization header — the AI will fetch your brain context securely
integrations.connect_existing: You already have Connect keys. Use the full key (shown once at creation) with the Authorization header.
integrations.connect_hint: Auth header format: Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_FULL_KEY
integrations.delete_confirm_text: Are you sure you want to permanently delete
integrations.generate_hint: Creates an API key with connect.read scope and gives you your personal URL.
integrations.mcp_desc: Full read/write via MCP. The best integration for AI coding tools.
integrations.mcp_cursor_path: Add to .cursor/mcp.json in your project or ~/.cursor/mcp.json globally:
integrations.mcp_claudecode_path: Add to .mcp.json in your project root:
integrations.mcp_restart: Restart your AI tool. OneBrain tools will appear automatically.
integrations.mcp_tools_list: get_user_context, search_memory, write_memory, write_memory_batch, upsert_entity, get_project_context
integrations.mcp_step2: Add this to your MCP config file:
integrations.mcp_step3: Restart your AI tool. It now has full access to your brain.
integrations.api_desc: For custom integrations. Use the API to read and write memories from any application.
integrations.api_section_desc: For custom integrations or agent frameworks. Full programmatic access.
integrations.system_prompt_desc: For AI tools that don't support URLs. Copy this system prompt and paste it manually.
integrations.key_masked_hint: The full key was only shown once at creation. Create a new key if you need a copy.
integrations.next_steps_desc: Your API key is ready. Choose how you want to connect OneBrain to your AI tools:
integrations.next_step_mcp_desc: Cursor, Claude Desktop, Windsurf, Claude Code
integrations.scope_read_desc: AI can read your brain context
integrations.scope_write_desc: AI can read and write memories
integrations.sync_desc: All your AIs stay in sync automatically. New knowledge from one AI is available to all others.
integrations.trust_review_desc: New memories need your approval before they become active.
integrations.trust_trusted_desc: New memories are saved directly — no approval needed.
integrations.trust_default_hint: By default, every new connection uses "Review" mode — memories must be approved before they become active. "Trusted" mode must be enabled manually per key.
integrations.commands_desc: Control sync behavior during any conversation:
integrations.cmd_ignore: Stop syncing new information in this conversation.
integrations.cmd_status: Check sync status and items synced.
integrations.platform_claude_desc: Full read/write via MCP protocol. Recommended for Claude and IDE users.
integrations.platform_chatgpt_desc: Create a Custom GPT with OneBrain Actions for automatic sync.
integrations.platform_chatgpt_step1: Go to ChatGPT → Create a GPT → Configure
integrations.platform_chatgpt_step3: Save. Your GPT now reads and writes to OneBrain automatically.
integrations.platform_gemini_desc: Create a Gem with your OneBrain context as system instructions.
integrations.platform_gemini_step2: In 'Instructions', paste your brain context (use the curl command to fetch it)
integrations.platform_gemini_step3: Gemini will use your brain context in every conversation.
integrations.platform_agents_desc: Direct REST API for any agent framework or custom script.
integrations.tool_get_context: Full brain context: profile, memories, entities, projects
integrations.tool_search: Search and filter memories by type and status
integrations.tool_write: Create a single memory (fact, preference, decision, ...)
integrations.tool_batch: Create multiple memories at once (1-10, dedup included)
integrations.tool_entity: Create or update an entity (person, place, org, ...)
integrations.tool_project: Get project details or list active projects
integrations.bookmarklet_desc: One click injects your brain context into any AI chat — works everywhere without plugins or paid plans.
integrations.bookmarklet_drag_hint: Drag this button to your bookmarks bar:
integrations.bookmarklet_drag_title: Drag to bookmarks bar to install
integrations.bookmarklet_step1: Drag the button above into your browser's bookmarks bar.
integrations.bookmarklet_step2: Open any supported AI chat (ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, etc.).
integrations.bookmarklet_step3: Click the bookmarklet — your brain context is injected into the input field automatically.
integrations.bookmarklet_note: On first use you'll be asked for your API key. It's stored locally in your browser — never sent to third parties.
integrations.next_step_bookmarklet_desc: ChatGPT, Claude Web, Grok, Perplexity, Gemini

admin.title: Admin Dashboard
admin.access_denied: You do not have permission to access this page.
admin.encryption_desc: Encrypt all existing unencrypted memories with AES-256-GCM.

landing.hero_subtitle: OneBrain remembers who you are, what you know, and what you're working on. Any AI assistant gets personalized context — automatically.
landing.features_title: Everything your AI needs to know you
landing.features_subtitle: A structured, secure memory system that makes every AI conversation personal.
landing.feature_memory_desc: Store facts, preferences, goals, decisions, experiences, and skills. Organized by type, scored by confidence, and always up to date.
landing.feature_ai_desc: Works with any LLM — Claude, GPT, Gemini, local models. Connect via REST API or MCP protocol. No vendor lock-in.
landing.feature_privacy_desc: Your data stays protected. EU/GLOBAL region isolation, GDPR-compliant, TLS-encrypted in transit. Secure hosted SaaS with full data sovereignty.
landing.feature_context_desc: Token-budgeted context delivery. 4 scopes from quick lookups (500 tokens) to deep analysis (8K tokens). Identity never dropped.
landing.feature_mcp_desc: Native Model Context Protocol support. Connect to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible tool with a single config.
landing.feature_merge_desc: No ML, no embeddings. Duplicate detection, conflict resolution, and confidence scoring — all explainable and auditable.
landing.feature_agents_desc: Not just for people. AI agents store and share their knowledge through OneBrain too — sync once, available everywhere. No more starting from scratch.
landing.step1_title: Sign up and create your brain
landing.step1_desc: Magic link login — no password needed. Your brain profile stores your identity, traits, and preferences.
landing.step2_desc: Extract memories from conversations, write them manually, or import knowledge from any source.
landing.step3_desc: Create an API key, configure your MCP server, and every AI assistant instantly knows who you are.
landing.step4_desc: Your AI reads your context before responding. No more repeating yourself — every conversation picks up where you left off.
landing.api_subtitle: RESTful endpoints with consistent JSON envelopes, cursor-based pagination, and Zod-validated inputs.
landing.sdk_subtitle: Install the official SDK or connect via MCP — zero config needed.
landing.agents_subtitle: Your agent registers itself and gets an API key instantly — no manual setup required.
landing.agents_step1_desc: One POST request creates an account + API key.
landing.agents_step2_desc: Read context, write memories — all via REST API.
landing.agents_step3_desc: One account per agent name. Store the key securely — it is shown only once.
landing.feature_deeprecall_desc: Find by meaning, not just words. Hybrid search combines semantic vectors with keyword matching for the most relevant results.
landing.feature_skillforge_desc: Your brain gets smarter with every session. Patterns are automatically extracted, scored, and injected into AI context.
landing.feature_brainpulse_desc: Your brain comes to you. Scheduled and event-triggered briefings delivered via email, webhook, or in-app.
landing.beta_banner: BETA — This project is in beta. Use at your own risk.
landing.disclaimer: This website was created solely to demonstrate and test the OneBrain project. There are no paid plans. We assume no liability for data loss, availability, or any damages arising from the use of this service.

consent.message: We use cookies for essential site functionality. Optional cookies help us improve the experience. You can change your preferences at any time.

impressum.title: Legal Notice (Impressum)
impressum.provider_text: OneBrain — AI Memory Layer. The operator details will be updated for the respective deployment.
impressum.responsible_text: The operator of this service is responsible for content according to applicable law.
impressum.dispute_text: We are neither willing nor obligated to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.overview_text: This Privacy Policy informs you pursuant to Art. 13 and 14 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation, GDPR) about the processing of your personal data when using OneBrain (hereinafter "Service"). OneBrain is an AI memory layer that stores user preferences, knowledge, decisions, and projects in a structured system and delivers optimized context to AI assistants via REST API or MCP protocol. The Service is offered both as a hosted SaaS solution and for self-hosting. This policy applies to both deployment modes.
privacy.controller_text:
The data controller within the meaning of the GDPR is:

OneBrain Project
Email: legal@onebrain.rocks

Data Protection Officer: datenschutz@onebrain.rocks
privacy.data_collection_text: We process only data that is necessary for providing the Service or that you voluntarily share with us. The principle of data minimization pursuant to Art. 5(1)(c) GDPR applies. The following sections detail each data category.
privacy.data_collection_account_text: Upon registration, we collect your email address as a unique identifier. You may optionally provide a display name. When using password authentication, your password is stored exclusively as an bcrypt hash — the plaintext password is never persisted. When using OAuth (Google, Apple, GitHub), we receive only the email address and provider ID you have authorized. When two-factor authentication (TOTP) is enabled, the TOTP secret is optionally encrypted with AES-256-GCM.
privacy.data_collection_memory_text: The core of the Service consists of storing your memories, entities, projects, and brain profile. This data is actively entered by you or generated by AI extraction from texts you provide. Memory data can optionally be encrypted with AES-256-GCM per tenant (MEMORY_ENCRYPTION_KEY). Access is strictly limited to your user account.
privacy.data_collection_technical_text: Each time you access the Service, the following data is automatically processed: IP address (masked in logs per GDPR), timestamp, HTTP method and path, user agent, request ID for traceability. Session data is processed as JWT tokens (httpOnly, secure, SameSite) and refresh tokens (hashed value in database). IP addresses are automatically masked in audit logs and never stored in plaintext.
privacy.data_collection_payment_text: For paid plans, payment processing is handled exclusively by Stripe Inc. We do not receive or store credit card numbers or bank details. Stripe transmits only a customer ID, payment status, and invoice information to us. See Section 7 for details.
privacy.data_collection_agent_text: AI agents can create accounts automatically via the provisioning endpoint. A synthetic email address is generated for agents (agent-[uuid]@agents.onebrain.local) that is not associated with any natural person. Agent accounts are marked with the type 'agent' and are subject to the same access restrictions as user accounts.
privacy.legal_basis_text:
Your personal data is processed on the following legal bases:

a) Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR): Processing of account data, memory data, session data, and API keys for providing the Service pursuant to our Terms of Service.

b) Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR): Processing of technical data for IT security, fraud prevention, abuse detection, rate limiting, and system stability. Our interest prevails as we process only minimally required data and mask IP addresses.

c) Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR): Setting optional statistics and marketing cookies, sending BrainPulse briefings via email. You may withdraw your consent at any time with effect for the future.

d) Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR): Retention of invoice data pursuant to tax law retention periods (6 or 10 years).
privacy.retention_text:
We store your data only as long as necessary for the respective purpose:

- Account data: Lifetime of the account + 30 days after deletion
- Memory data: Lifetime of the account + 30 days after deletion. Configurable TTL (default: 365 days) for automatic cleanup
- Session data: 30 days after token expiry
- Usage data and API activity: 24 months
- Audit logs: 90 days
- Consent records: 3 years (proof obligation)
- Invoice data: 10 years (commercial law retention requirement)
- Magic link tokens: 15 minutes, then automatically deleted

After the retention period expires, data is automatically and irrevocably deleted. The retention job runs every 6 hours by default.
privacy.cookies_text:
We use the following cookie categories:

a) Necessary cookies (always active): Authentication cookies (httpOnly, secure, SameSite=Lax), consent cookie for storing your cookie preferences. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.

b) Statistics cookies (opt-in): Only with explicit consent via the cookie banner. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.

c) Marketing cookies (opt-in): Only with explicit consent via the cookie banner. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.

You can change your cookie settings at any time via the cookie banner. Consent is logged with timestamp and version.
privacy.third_parties_text: We share your personal data with third parties only insofar as necessary for providing the Service or with your consent. Data processing agreements pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR are in place with all processors.
privacy.third_parties_hetzner_text: Hosting and infrastructure. Server location: Nuremberg, Germany (EU). Hetzner processes data exclusively within the scope of data processing. Privacy information: https://www.hetzner.com/legal/privacy-policy
privacy.third_parties_stripe_text: Payment processing for paid plans. Stripe is certified under the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework. Only payment-relevant data is transmitted (email, payment amounts). Privacy information: https://stripe.com/privacy
privacy.third_parties_resend_text: Transactional emails (magic links, BrainPulse briefings). Email address and email content are transmitted. Servers in EU/USA.
privacy.third_parties_openai_text: For the DeepRecall feature (semantic search), memory contents may optionally be processed as embedding vectors. This only occurs when you activate the feature. Texts are sent to the embedding API, are not stored there, and are used exclusively to generate numerical vectors.
privacy.data_transfers_text: Data is principally processed within the EU/EEA (server location: Germany). Insofar as data processors transfer data to the USA (Stripe, optionally OpenAI), this is done on the basis of the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (EU Commission adequacy decision) or on the basis of EU Standard Contractual Clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR.
privacy.rights_text: You have the following rights regarding your personal data. To exercise them, send an email to datenschutz@onebrain.rocks or use the corresponding functions in your account settings.
privacy.rights_access_text: You have the right to obtain information about the personal data we process. You can download your data at any time via the export function in your account settings as a JSON file.
privacy.rights_rectification_text: You have the right to have inaccurate personal data corrected. You can edit your profile, memories, and all other data at any time via the dashboard.
privacy.rights_erasure_text: You have the right to erasure of your data ("right to be forgotten"). You can delete individual memories, reset your entire brain, or completely delete your account. After account deletion, all data is irrevocably removed within 30 days. GDPR endpoints (data export, account deletion) are available via the API.
privacy.rights_portability_text: You have the right to receive your data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format. The export function provides all memory data, entities, projects, and your brain profile as JSON.
privacy.rights_objection_text: You have the right to object at any time, on grounds relating to your particular situation, to the processing of your data based on Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
privacy.rights_complaint_text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of your habitual residence, place of work, or place of the alleged infringement.
privacy.security_text:
We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data pursuant to Art. 32 GDPR:

- Encryption: TLS 1.2+ for all data transmissions, optional AES-256-GCM encryption for memory data and TOTP secrets
- Access control: Strict per-user data isolation, API keys with scope restrictions, role-based access control for administrators
- Authentication: Password hashing with bcrypt, JWT tokens with short lifetime (15 minutes), httpOnly refresh cookies, optional TOTP two-factor authentication
- Network security: Helmet.js security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options), rate limiting on all endpoints, CORS whitelisting in production
- Monitoring: Audit logging of all security-relevant actions, IP masking in logs, no personal data in log output
- Infrastructure: Docker containers with non-root user, memory limits, regular security updates
privacy.children_text: The Service is not directed at persons under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from persons under 16. If we become aware that data of a child under 16 has been stored, we will delete it immediately.
privacy.changes_text: We reserve the right to update this Privacy Policy as needed, for example due to changes in the Service or legal requirements. The current version is always available at https://onebrain.rocks/datenschutz. For material changes, we will notify you by email or through a notice in the Service.
privacy.contact_text:
For questions about data protection, to exercise your data subject rights, or for complaints, please contact:

OneBrain Project
Email: datenschutz@onebrain.rocks

We will respond to your request without delay, at the latest within one month (Art. 12(3) GDPR).

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.scope_text: These Terms of Service (hereinafter "Terms") govern the contractual relationship between the OneBrain project (hereinafter "Provider") and the user (hereinafter "User" or "you") of the OneBrain service (hereinafter "Service"). The Service comprises an AI memory layer for structured storage and delivery of user knowledge to AI assistants. By registering and creating an account, you agree to these Terms. Deviating or supplementary terms of the User shall not become part of the contract unless the Provider expressly agrees in writing.
terms.definitions_text:
"Service": The OneBrain platform including web app, REST API, MCP server, SDKs, and associated documentation.
"User": Any natural or legal person who creates an account and uses the Service.
"Agent": An automated software system (AI agent) that accesses the Service via the API.
"Brain": The entirety of stored memories, entities, projects, and profile information of a User.
"API Key": A machine-readable access token with defined permissions (scopes) for programmatic access.
"Memory Data": Content entered by the User or generated by AI extraction (memories, entities, projects, brain profile).
terms.account_text: Use of the Service requires the creation of a user account. You must provide truthful information during registration. Each natural person may maintain only one account. The minimum age for use is 16 years. You are obligated to keep your access credentials (password, API keys, TOTP secret) confidential and protect them from third-party access. You are liable for all activities conducted through your account unless you are not responsible for the unauthorized use. In case of suspected abuse, you are obligated to notify us immediately.
terms.account_agent_text: AI agents can create accounts automatically via the provisioning endpoint. The same Terms apply to each agent account. The operator of the agent (the person or organization using the provisioning key) is responsible for the agent's actions, including stored content and compliance with these Terms. Only one account per agent name is permitted.
terms.usage_text: You may use the Service exclusively for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree not to use the Service for storing, processing, or distributing content that violates applicable law.
terms.usage_prohibited_text:
The following are expressly prohibited:
- Storage of illegal, glorifying violence, discriminatory, or pornographic content
- Infringement of copyrights, trademark rights, or other rights of third parties
- Attempts to impair the security, integrity, or availability of the Service (including DDoS, injection, reverse engineering)
- Automated scanning or scraping beyond documented API limits
- Resale or sublicensing of access without written permission
- Use of the Service for spam, phishing, or social engineering
- Circumvention of rate limits, authentication mechanisms, or access restrictions
terms.ip_text: The Service, including source code, design, documentation, and trademarks, is the intellectual property of the Provider and is available under an open-source license (as published on GitHub). Your memory data remains your property. You grant the Provider a simple, temporally and spatially unlimited right of use to your data insofar as this is necessary for providing the Service (storage, processing, delivery via the API). Your data will not be used beyond service provision.
terms.data_protection_text: The protection of your personal data is of central concern to us. For details, please refer to our Privacy Policy at https://onebrain.rocks/datenschutz. The Privacy Policy is an integral part of these Terms.
terms.availability_text: The Provider endeavors to ensure high availability of the Service but does not guarantee a specific availability rate unless agreed upon in a separate Service Level Agreement (SLA). Maintenance work, updates, and security patches may lead to temporary restrictions. The Provider will inform about planned maintenance in advance where possible. The Provider is not liable for outages caused by force majeure, third-party disruptions, or network issues.
terms.payment_text: The Service is offered in a free basic plan and paid plans. Current prices and features are available on the pricing page (https://onebrain.rocks/pricing). Paid plans are billed monthly or annually in advance. Payment is processed exclusively through Stripe Inc. The Provider does not issue invoices directly; invoicing is handled through Stripe. All prices include statutory VAT where applicable.
terms.payment_refund_text: You may cancel paid plans at any time effective at the end of the current billing period. After cancellation, access remains until the end of the paid period, after which the account is automatically downgraded to the free plan. No pro-rata refund of amounts already paid for the current period will be made unless the Provider has materially and persistently failed to perform the Service in accordance with the contract.
terms.api_keys_text: API keys are confidential access credentials and must be treated like passwords. The full key is displayed only once upon creation and cannot be retrieved thereafter. You are obligated to immediately revoke compromised keys. The Provider is not liable for damages arising from the misuse of disclosed API keys. API keys can be created with expiration dates and scope restrictions. Compliance with rate limits is mandatory.
terms.termination_text: You may delete your account at any time without giving reasons via account settings or by emailing legal@onebrain.rocks. After deletion, all your personal data and memory data will be irrevocably deleted within 30 days, unless statutory retention obligations apply.
terms.termination_by_us_title: 10.1 Termination and Suspension by the Provider
terms.termination_by_us_text: The Provider is entitled to temporarily suspend or permanently terminate accounts in case of violation of these Terms. In case of serious violations (particularly Section 4.1), termination without prior warning is permissible. The Provider will inform the User of the reasons for suspension or termination insofar as legally permissible.
terms.liability_text: The Provider is liable without limitation for damages arising from injury to life, body, or health, as well as for intentional misconduct and gross negligence. For slight negligence, the Provider is liable only in case of breach of material contractual obligations (cardinal obligations); in this case, liability is limited to foreseeable, contract-typical damages.
terms.liability_limitation_text: The Provider's liability for slight negligence in breach of material contractual obligations is limited to the amount the User has paid to the Provider in the last 12 months, but not less than EUR 100. Liability for indirect damages, consequential damages, lost profits, data loss, or damages from unavailability of the Service is excluded for slight negligence to the extent permitted by law. The Service is provided "as is". The Provider does not guarantee uninterrupted, error-free, or complete availability of the Service.
terms.indemnification_text: The User shall indemnify and hold harmless the Provider from all third-party claims arising from unlawful use of the Service by the User or from content stored by the User. This includes reasonable costs of legal defense.
terms.force_majeure_text: The Provider shall not be liable for non-performance or delayed performance of its obligations insofar as attributable to circumstances beyond its reasonable control (force majeure), including natural disasters, war, terrorism, pandemics, strikes, governmental orders, failures of telecommunications networks, or energy providers.
terms.changes_text: The Provider reserves the right to amend these Terms with reasonable notice. Changes will be communicated to the User by email or through a notice in the Service at least 30 days before taking effect. If the User does not object to the changes within the notice period and continues to use the Service, the new Terms shall be deemed accepted. The right to object will be expressly stated in the change notification.
terms.severability_text: Should any provision of these Terms be or become invalid or unenforceable, the validity of the remaining provisions shall not be affected. In place of the invalid or unenforceable provision, that valid and enforceable provision shall apply which comes closest to the economic purpose of the invalid provision.
terms.jurisdiction_text: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG). For disputes arising from or in connection with these Terms, the Provider's registered office shall be the exclusive place of jurisdiction, to the extent permitted by law. For consumers, the mandatory provisions of consumer law of their state of residence shall apply insofar as they are more favorable.

help.dashboard_welcome: Your personal dashboard — see your stats, recent memories, and quick links at a glance.
help.dashboard_get_started: Follow these steps to set up your brain profile and start building your knowledge base.
help.dashboard_quick_actions: Shortcuts to the most common tasks: add memories, edit your profile, or connect an AI.
help.dashboard_recent_memories: The latest memories you have added or imported — click to see all.
help.brain_title: Manage your brain profile — this is how AI assistants will understand who you are.
help.brain_summary: Write a short description about yourself, your intention, and what you do with AI. AI assistants use this information as context.
help.brain_summary_label: Describe yourself, your goals, and what you use AI for.
help.brain_traits: Define traits as key-value pairs, e.g. 'Role: Developer' or 'Experience: 10 years'.
help.brain_preferences: Define your preferences, e.g. 'Language: TypeScript' or 'Style: pragmatic'.
help.brain_scope: Choose how much context is sent to the AI — from brief to comprehensive.
help.brain_stats: An overview of how much knowledge your brain currently holds.
help.brain_context_preview: Preview the exact text that gets sent to AI assistants as your personal context.
help.memory_title: Browse, search, edit, and organize all your stored memories. You can also import and export.
help.entities_title: People, places, tools, and concepts extracted from your memories. Manage your knowledge graph.
help.projects_title: Group your memories into projects. Track goals, decisions, and progress in one place.
help.integrations_title: Connect OneBrain to your AI tools — Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, or any agent via API.
help.integrations_connect: The easiest way to connect: generate a single URL and paste it into your AI tool.
help.integrations_sync: Advanced two-way sync protocol with trust levels and user commands for fine control.
help.integrations_platforms: Step-by-step setup guides for each supported platform.
help.integrations_api: Use the REST API directly to read context and write memories from any application.
help.integrations_system_prompt: Export your brain context as a system prompt to paste into any AI chat.
help.ingest_title: Import knowledge into your brain from text, files, URLs, or AI-assisted extraction.
help.ingest_knowledge_overview: A summary of all knowledge currently stored in your brain.
help.ingest_candidates: Review AI-extracted memory candidates before they are saved to your brain.
help.ingest_manual: Manually add a single memory with title, content, and type.
help.ingest_bulk: Paste multiple items at once to add them in bulk.
help.ingest_ai_extract: Let AI analyze your text and automatically extract structured memories.
help.ingest_upload: Upload files (JSON, CSV, TXT) to import memories in bulk.
help.ingest_url: Provide a URL and let AI extract knowledge from the web page.
help.ingest_chat: Have a conversation with AI to naturally build memories from dialogue.
help.api_keys_title: Create and manage API keys for programmatic access to your OneBrain account.
help.billing_title: View your current plan, manage your subscription, and download invoices.
help.billing_current_plan: Your active subscription plan and its status.
help.billing_invoices: Download past invoices and see payment history.
help.usage_title: Track how you use OneBrain — requests, tokens, and activity over time.
help.usage_daily_activity: A chart showing your daily request volume.
help.usage_by_type: Breakdown of usage by event type (query, ingest, sync, etc.).
help.sessions_title: View all active login sessions and revoke access from devices you no longer use.
help.shares_title: Create shareable links to your brain context. Control scope and expiration.
help.referrals_title: Invite friends to OneBrain and earn rewards when they sign up.
help.referrals_referred_users: A list of all users you have referred and their status.
help.agents_title: All AI tools and agents syncing with your brain — see activity, sync status, and pending knowledge.
help.agents_detail: Detailed view per agent — sync activity, trust level, and configuration. Click an agent to manage.
help.agents_config: Configure the agent's name, trust level, rate limit, and allowed scopes.
help.agents_activity: Recent API calls made by this agent — action type, status, and response time.
help.search_title: Search your memories using keyword matching, vector similarity, or hybrid mode. Adjust the fusion weight to control the balance between exact matches and semantic meaning. View embedding coverage and re-index missing items.
help.skills_title: Skills are patterns your brain has learned from repeated memories and usage. Review confidence scores, see how often a skill was applied, and give feedback to improve future suggestions. Requires Pro or Team plan.
help.briefings_title: BrainPulse sends you proactive briefings based on your brain profile — morning summaries, evening recaps, or event-triggered updates. Configure timezone, quiet hours, and delivery channels.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about OneBrain — explained so anyone can understand it.
faq.what_is_a: OneBrain is like a brain for your AI tools. It remembers who you are, what you like, what you know, and what you're working on. When you talk to ChatGPT, Claude, or any AI — it already knows your stuff. No more repeating yourself.
faq.who_for_a: For anyone who uses AI tools. Developers, students, writers, teams — if you talk to AI and want it to remember things between conversations, OneBrain is for you. It also works for AI agents that need shared memory.
faq.free_a: Yes! There's a free plan with enough memory for personal use. If you need more, there are paid plans with higher limits. Check the pricing page for details.
faq.open_source_a: Yes, the core is open source on GitHub. You can self-host it on your own server or use the hosted version at onebrain.rocks. Your choice.
faq.what_memory_a: A memory is a piece of information about you — like 'I prefer dark mode' or 'I'm working on a React app'. Each memory has a type (fact, preference, goal, skill, etc.), a confidence score, and tags. Your AI uses these memories to give you better answers.
faq.how_add_a: Three ways: type them manually in the dashboard, paste a conversation and let AI extract the important parts, or use the API/MCP server so your AI tools save memories automatically as you chat.
faq.memory_types_q: What types of memories can I store?
faq.memory_types_a: Facts (things about you), preferences (what you like), goals (what you're working toward), decisions (choices you've made), experiences (things you've done), and skills (what you're good at). Each type helps the AI understand you better.
faq.memory_limit_q: Is there a limit on memories?
faq.memory_limit_a: Depends on your plan. The free plan has a generous limit for personal use. Paid plans give you more. Check your usage dashboard to see where you're at.
faq.which_ai_q: Which AI tools work with OneBrain?
faq.which_ai_a: Basically all of them. ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Cursor, any local LLM — if it can make an HTTP request or use MCP, it works. There are SDKs for Node.js, Python, Laravel, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Vercel AI, and more.
faq.ai_agnostic_q: Do I need to pick one AI? Or does it work with all of them?
faq.ai_agnostic_a: It works with all of them at the same time. That's the whole point — you save your knowledge once, and every AI tool you use gets access. Switch between ChatGPT and Claude mid-day? Both know your stuff.
faq.mcp_what_q: What is MCP and do I need it?
faq.mcp_what_a: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard way for AI tools to read external data. If you use Cursor, Claude Desktop, or Windsurf — MCP lets them read your OneBrain automatically. Just add one config line and you're done. It's optional but super handy.
faq.context_engine_q: What does the Smart Context Engine do?
faq.context_engine_a: It picks the right memories for each AI request. Instead of dumping everything, it sends only what's relevant and fits within the token budget. 4 levels: quick (500 tokens), standard (2K), deep (4K), full (8K). Your identity is always included.
faq.what_agent_q: What is an AI agent in OneBrain?
faq.what_agent_a: An AI agent is a bot or program that uses OneBrain's API. Think of it as an automated assistant — it can read your context, store new knowledge, and work on your behalf. Each agent gets its own API key and permissions.
faq.agent_register_q: How does an agent get an API key?
faq.agent_register_a: Easy. The agent sends a POST request to /v1/agents/register with its name and description. No login needed. It gets back an API key with read-only access instantly. Alternatively, an admin can create an invite link and share it with the agent.
faq.invite_how_a: Admins create invite links from the dashboard. Each link has a unique code, optional expiration, and a use limit. Agents visit /invite/CODE, fill in their name and description, and receive an API key. The admin controls how many agents can use each link and when it expires.
faq.invite_access_q: Can I choose read-only or read+write for invite links?
faq.invite_access_a: Yes. When creating an invite link, the admin selects the access level: read-only (3 scopes: connect.read, brain.read, entity.read) or read+write (8 scopes including write access to memory, entities, and brain). This is set per invite link so different links can have different permissions.
faq.agent_scopes_q: What can agents do with their API key?
faq.agent_scopes_a: Self-registered agents start with read-only access: they can read your brain context, memory, and entities. Admins can upgrade them to also write memories and sync data. Each key has specific scopes so you control exactly what each agent can do.
faq.agent_trust_q: What is an agent's trust level?
faq.agent_trust_a: New agents start at 'review' — meaning their actions are tracked and they have limited permissions. An admin can upgrade them to 'trusted' which gives them more access. Think of it like a probation period for bots.
faq.deeprecall_a: DeepRecall is smart search. Instead of just matching keywords, it understands what you mean. Search for 'that project I did with React' and it finds the right memories even if you never used those exact words. It combines meaning-based and keyword search.
faq.skillforge_a: SkillForge watches how you use your AI tools and automatically spots patterns. If you always debug code the same way or prefer a certain writing style, it picks that up and turns it into a 'skill'. These skills then get injected into your AI context so it works your way.
faq.skillforge_how_q: Do I need to set up SkillForge?
faq.skillforge_how_a: Nope. It works automatically in the background. The more you use OneBrain, the smarter it gets at spotting your patterns. You can see your extracted skills in the dashboard and disable any you don't want.
faq.brainpulse_a: BrainPulse sends you briefings — like a smart summary of what's new in your brain. You can get daily digests, weekly health reports, or event-triggered alerts. Delivered via email, webhook, or in the app.
faq.merge_engine_q: What is the Deterministic Merge Engine?
faq.merge_engine_a: When you or your AI tools add conflicting information, the merge engine figures out what to keep. It detects duplicates, resolves conflicts, and scores confidence — all with clear rules you can understand. No black-box AI magic.
faq.gdpr_q: Is my data safe? Is OneBrain GDPR compliant?
faq.gdpr_a: Yes and yes. Servers are in Germany (EU), data is encrypted at rest, you can export or delete all your data anytime, and there's a proper cookie consent banner. OneBrain is fully GDPR/DSGVO compliant.
faq.data_where_a: On servers in Germany, operated by Hetzner. Your data never leaves the EU unless you explicitly choose a different region. Everything is encrypted.
faq.data_delete_a: Yes, completely. Go to your dashboard, click delete account, and everything is wiped — your brain, memories, API keys, all of it. As required by GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure).
faq.encryption_a: Yes. Data is encrypted at rest in the database, and all traffic uses TLS (HTTPS). API keys are hashed with SHA-256 — even we can't see them.
faq.cookies_a: Only essential cookies by default (for login sessions). Statistics and marketing cookies are opt-in via the cookie banner. You choose what you allow.
faq.self_host_q: Can I run OneBrain on my own server?
faq.self_host_a: Yes! Clone the repo, run docker compose up, done. There are scripts for Hetzner VPS deployment with auto-HTTPS. You own your data 100%.
faq.api_limits_a: Yes. Authenticated users get 600 requests per minute. Write operations have lower limits. If you need more, paid plans offer higher quotas. The API returns clear rate-limit headers so your code can handle it.
faq.sdks_a: Node.js/TypeScript, Python, Laravel/PHP, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Vercel AI SDK, OpenClaw, and an MCP Server. All open source on GitHub.
faq.languages_a: The interface is available in English, German, and Spanish. Adding new languages is easy — just copy a JSON file. Your memories can be in any language.

agent_register.success: Agent registered successfully. Your API key has read-only access (connect.read, brain.read, entity.read). Contact an admin to upgrade scopes.
agent_register.name_taken: An agent with this name already exists. Choose a different name.
agent_register.rate_limited: Too many registration attempts from this IP. Try again later.
agent_register.ip_limit_exceeded: Maximum daily registrations from this IP reached. Try again in 24 hours.
agent_register.validation_error: Invalid registration data. Provide name (2-100 chars), description (10-1000 chars), and optional contactUrl.
agent_register.trust_level_info: Your agent starts at 'review' trust level with a 90-day key. An admin can upgrade your trust level and scopes.

invite.page_subtitle: Use your invite code to register an agent and get an API key.
invite.page_subtitle_read: This invite grants read-only access (view context, memories, and entities).
invite.page_subtitle_readwrite: This invite grants read + write access (view and create memories, entities, and brain data).
invite.desc_input_placeholder: What does your agent do? (min. 10 characters)
invite.invalid_code: This invite code is invalid or expired.
invite.success_text: Your agent has been created. Save your API key now — it will not be shown again.
invite.key_warning: Save this key in a secure location. It cannot be retrieved later.
invite.about_desc: OneBrain is a persistent AI memory layer. It stores identity, knowledge, preferences, and decisions for humans and AI agents — and delivers optimized context to any AI tool. Your data stays consistent across sessions and tools.
invite.about_context_desc: Get a token-budgeted summary of the user's brain — profile, memories, entities, and projects — optimized for your AI prompt.
invite.about_memory_desc: Store facts, preferences, decisions, goals, experiences, and skills. Full CRUD with types, tags, and status tracking.
invite.about_search_desc: Find memories by meaning, not just keywords. Hybrid search combines semantic vectors with keyword matching.
invite.about_entities_desc: Track people, tools, concepts, and projects. Link them to memories for a connected knowledge graph.
invite.about_connect_desc: A single URL that returns a ready-to-use system prompt with the user's full context. Add it to any AI assistant.
invite.guide_intro: Use your API key to access OneBrain. Here are the most important endpoints:
invite.guide_context: Get AI-optimized context (your brain summary for AI tools):
invite.guide_connect: Get your Connect URL (system prompt for AI assistants):
invite.guide_docs_hint: The OpenAPI spec lists all available endpoints, parameters, and response formats. Use it to discover everything OneBrain offers.
invite.global_enabled_desc: Invite-based agent registration is currently enabled. Agents can register using valid invite codes.
invite.global_disabled_desc: Invite-based agent registration is currently disabled. No invite codes will work.
invite.label: Label
invite.desc_placeholder: Internal note about this invite link
